untranslatable_reason: This signal requires fundamental data sorting (Earnings-to-Price ratio and 5-year sales growth) and portfolio construction across 9 independent sorts. These are not technical indicators but rather require access to fundamental financial statements and cross-sectional portfolio formation logic. The allowed indicator set contains only price-based, volume-based, and technical indicators. Fundamental ratio calculations and multi-dimensional stock sorting cannot be expressed in the provided technical indicator framework.
translation_notes: Signal SSRN_227016_20 is a classic academic factor-based portfolio sort strategy (Reversion factor study, likely Chopra et al. 1992). It requires: (1) calculation of Earnings-to-Price ratio from fundamental data, (2) calculation of 5-year weighted average sales growth from fundamental data, (3) independent percentile-based sorting into 3 groups each, (4) portfolio construction at intersections of these sorts, (5) annual rebalancing in April, (6) 5-year holding period. This is a fundamental/cross-sectional strategy incompatible with single-security technical analysis rules. Recommend implementing in a portfolio backtester with fundamental data access rather than a price-action rules engine.
